Title: A CysB regulator positively regulates cysteine synthesis, expression of type III secretion system genes, and pathogenicity in Ralstonia solanacearum

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LysR-type transcriptional regulator (LTTR); N-terminal helix-turn-helix DNA-binding domain and C-terminal substrate-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sulfur-containing amino acid required for bacterial growth and contributes to pathogenic fitness in planta
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial cytoplasm; availability inside host plant tissues influences bacterial proliferation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: cysteine biosynthesis and broader virulence-associated regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively regulates cysU/cysI regulons for cysteine synthesis; positively regulates prhG and thereby hrpB and T3SS gene expression; positively affects xpsR expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: cysU; cysW; cysA; cysI; cysH; cysD; cysN; prhG; hrpB; popA; xpsR
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: sulfur/cysteine nutritional status inferred; hrp-inducing minimal medium and in planta conditions were used to reveal regulatory effects
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator for cysteine synthesis in R. solanacearum; also a novel positive regulator of T3SS expression via the PrhG-HrpB pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cysteine synthesis, in vitro and in planta growth, T3SS expression, EPS production, swimming motility, and pathogenicity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in-frame deletion mutant; chromosomal complementation; cysteine supplementation rescue; promoter-lacZYA reporter assays in vitro and in planta; growth assays in minimal medium and host plants; virulence assays; EPS quantification; swimming motility assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of cysB caused cysteine auxotrophy: mutants failed to grow in minimal medium and growth was fully restored by complementation or exogenous cysteine. 2. cysB positively activated cysU and cysI regulon expression, supporting its role in cysteine synthesis. 3. cysB positively regulated prhG, hrpB, and popA/T3SS expression in vitro and in planta, independent of growth deficiency under nutrient-limited conditions. 4. cysB was required for efficient proliferation in tomato stems and tobacco leaves, EPS production, swimming motility, and full pathogenicity.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Functional effects detected in rich medium, hrp-inducing minimal medium, and in planta; prhG regulation observed in both rich and minimal media
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: hrp-inducing minimal medium (Hoagland medium with 2% sucrose); cysteine supplementation (20 mM in medium or inoculum; 0.1 mM for tomato cuttings); tobacco leaf and tomato stem infection assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of cysB severely reduces growth in minimal medium, in planta proliferation, EPS production, swimming motility, and virulence; no clear biofilm defect reported
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter for complementation; cysU, cysI, popA, hrpB, hrpG, prhG, prhN, phcA, and xpsR promoters used in lacZYA reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antimicrobial target for reducing R. solanacearum virulence by simultaneously disrupting cysteine biosynthesis and T3SS regulation; useful for pathogen-control strategies rather than crop metabolic engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper demonstrates regulation through the PrhG-HrpB pathway, while direct DNA binding targets of CysB were not proven; Cbl, despite homology, showed no detectable role in these phenotypes.
